howItWorks.intro: This early retirement calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: FIRE Number = Annual Expenses / Withdrawal Rate
Years = Time for savings + growth to reach FIRE Number
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is the FIRE number?
faq.answer: Your FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) number is annual expenses divided by your safe withdrawal rate. At 4% withdrawal, you need 25× your annual expenses.

faq.question: What is the 4% rule?
faq.answer: The Trinity Study found that withdrawing 4% of a diversified portfolio annually (adjusted for inflation) has a very high success rate over 30 years. Some prefer 3.5% for extra safety.

faq.question: What about healthcare before 65?
faq.answer: Early retirees need private health insurance until Medicare at 65. Budget $500-$1,500/month per person via ACA marketplace plans.

faq.question: Can I really retire in my 30s or 40s?
faq.answer: Yes, if your savings rate is high enough. At a 50% savings rate, you can retire in about 17 years. At 70% savings rate, about 8.5 years. The math works.
